summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing for Caya Technologies enterprise site. All 15 pytest backend tests pass. All critical frontend flows (home, newsletter, contact form + validation, platform pages, insights, admin login/redirect, article CRUD → public visibility → delete, submissions list) pass. No blocking issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: Rate limiter uses ISO-string comparison ($gte with .isoformat()) which is fine lexicographically for UTC ISO but would break if timezone offset varies (currently UTC-only, acceptable).

CORS_ORIGINS defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True which is invalid per CORS spec when using cookies. Not an issue currently since frontend uses Bearer tokens, but should restrict origins in production.
seed_admin correctly rehashes password if changed (idempotent). Good.
JWT expires in 12h - reasonable. No refresh flow, so admin must re-login after 12h.
No brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook mentions 5-fail lockout). Rate limiting only covers /submissions and /newsletter, not /auth/login. Consider adding.
seed_data_creation: Backend seeds 3 articles + admin user on startup automatically. Test-created submissions and articles were cleaned up after tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py cover auth, submissions, newsletter, articles CRUD, and 401 protection. Rate limit is 8/60s per IP on /submissions and /newsletter - space out repeated test runs. Test data cleanup done via API after tests. All data-testid attributes are in place for admin and lead forms.
